projectName: Elementify Free + Advanced Scenario/Wizard Realignment v2

execution_recommendation.reason: This iteration adds bootstrap and stack-guidance design across product, wizard, and capability layers, which requires attended Ralph Loop execution with explicit review checkpoints.

tasks.title: Lock user-first ecosystem-honest positioning and Vamerli bridge
tasks.description: Define Elementify's explicit USP as a user-first, intent-led, ecosystem-honest 360-degree guidance layer for the Elementor stack, and document the future bridge to Vamerli.
acceptanceCriteria: The blueprint explicitly states that recommendations follow user interest and scenario fit before vendor or upsell logic
The positioning distinguishes Elementify from plugin-first or commercially-captured guidance layers
The relationship between Elementify and a future Vamerli brand presence and intelligence platform is documented clearly but without overclaiming present scope
This positioning becomes a source of truth for later scenario, wizard, and upgrade-guidance work

tasks.title: Define bootstrap scenarios and non-Elementor entry states
tasks.description: Extend the scenario map so Elementify supports users with only an idea, an existing non-Elementor WordPress site, or a partial stack that needs guided completion.
acceptanceCriteria: The scenario system includes entry states without Elementor installed
Bootstrap-from-idea, bootstrap-from-existing-WordPress, and stack-completion scenarios are documented
Each bootstrap scenario defines the desired Free baseline and deeper Advanced path
The scenario map remains consistent with the Free public value promise

tasks.title: Define intent-routing wizard contract
tasks.description: Model the front-door wizard that captures origin, intention, scope depth, constraints, and user posture and routes users into the correct Free or Advanced flow.
acceptanceCriteria: The intent wizard captures origin, intent, depth, and user posture
The routing model covers bootstrap, clean-up, refresh, optimization, extension, reduction, relaunch, and migration
The contract is understandable for both no-code-near and technical users
The output can be used as a source of truth for future wizard implementation

tasks.title: Define supported baseline stack profiles
tasks.description: Describe the supported Free and Advanced starting stacks, including Elementor Free, Hello Theme, and curated supported addon profiles.
acceptanceCriteria: A baseline Free stack profile is defined around WordPress, Elementor Free, Hello Theme, and supported addon profiles where appropriate
Advanced stack profiles define richer recommended combinations without collapsing into plugin inflation
The blueprint distinguishes recommendation, guided install, and automated execution honestly
Theme and addon recommendations remain curated and scenario-aware

tasks.title: Define modular skill and addon extension architecture
tasks.description: Describe how curated skill profiles and addon profiles extend the baseline stack without collapsing the product into unchecked extension sprawl.
acceptanceCriteria: The blueprint distinguishes baseline stack profiles from optional addon profiles and optional skill profiles
Skill profiles are described as scenario- and posture-aware operating layers
Addon profiles are described as curated, support-aware extensions rather than default plugin inflation
The resulting model remains consistent with the user-first, ecosystem-honest USP

tasks.title: Define scenario-based upgrade guidance
tasks.description: Model when and how Elementify should recommend Elementor Pro, Elementify Advanced, or selected supported pro plugins as a meaningful next step.
acceptanceCriteria: Upgrade guidance is tied to scenarios, not generic upsell prompts
The blueprint explains when Free is enough and when deeper stack upgrades make sense
Advanced is positioned as a workflow/productivity upgrade rather than a feature bundle
The design leaves room for supported pro plugin profiles without overcommitting vendor breadth

tasks.title: Rebuild the capability layer under the scenario/wizard model
tasks.description: Reset the capability model so it serves as a technical safety layer beneath the new bootstrap, scenario, and wizard design.
acceptanceCriteria: Capabilities are documented as technical boundaries instead of primary product concepts
The model supports stack-bootstrap, Elementor data, design system, theme structure, library, media, site context, plugin context, and governance domains
The model avoids permission inflation where no real boundary exists
A migration plan from the current mixed capability state is documented

tasks.title: Realign product surfaces to bootstrap-aware scenario model
tasks.description: Update Free and Advanced surfaces, docs, and future tool-routing assumptions so the repo matches the v2 scenario and wizard blueprint.
acceptanceCriteria: Existing product-surface docs are updated to reflect bootstrap-aware scenarios
Free is described as a 360-degree starting point where appropriate
Advanced is described as a deeper workflow and stack-shaping layer on the same scenario families
Public Free mirror constraints remain intact after the realignment
